channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iranian activist, Nobel prize winner Mohammadi released from jail for medical treatment
item.description: Human rights activist Narges Mohammadi, who is imprisoned in Iran on charges including spreading propaganda, had her jail term suspended to undergo medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's insurgency leader tours seized city of Aleppo, as fierce battles intensify near Hama
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The leader of Syria's most powerful insurgent group toured the seized city of Aleppo on Wednesday in a surprise visit — the first since the group captured large parts of the city over the weekend as fierce fighting intensifies in the government-led counter-offensive in northern Hama.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Austrian court rules that Ukrainian businessman Firtash can't be extradited to the US
item.description: An Austrian court has ruled that Ukrainian businessman Dymitro Firtash can’t be extradited to the U.S. in a long-running legal saga that centers on a corruption case related to an alleged plan to pay bribes in India.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NATO chief urges European allies to ramp up defense spending as Trump returns to White House
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte led a fresh push Wednesday for European countries to ramp up defense spending, a budget shortfall that President-elect Donald Trump used to berate U.S. allies during his first term in office, severely damaging trust.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean President Yoon's martial law declaration raises questions over his political future
item.description: President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU condemns reported Taliban move to suspend medical education for women and girls
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday condemned the Taliban for violating human rights and women's access to education after media reports that the Taliban's leader has ordered private and public institutions to stop providing medical courses for women and girls in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese court convicts Australian who says she was tricked into smuggling drugs
item.description: CHIBA, Japan (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday sentenced an Australian woman to six years in prison for smuggling amphetamines into the country, despite accepting her testimony that she was tricked as part of an online romance scam.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas and Fatah are near an agreement on who will oversee postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New UN aid chief vows 'ruthlessness' to prioritize spending as funding for world's crises shrinks
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The new head of the U.N. humanitarian aid agency says it will be “ruthless” when prioritizing how to spend money, a nod to challenges in fundraising for civilians in war zones like Gaza, Sudan, Syria and Ukraine. Tom Fletcher, a longtime British diplomat who took up the U.N.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shock martial law edict in South Korea follows chaotic recent history: A look at the crucial context
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — It may seem that the wild predawn scenes in Seoul, with hundreds of armed troops and frantic lawmakers storming South Korea's parliament building after the president suddenly declared martial law, came out of nowhere.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s opposition parties move to impeach president over sudden declaration of martial law
item.description: South Korea’s opposition parties moved to impeach the president over the shocking and short-lived declaration of martial law that drew heavily armed troops to encircle parliament.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes on Gaza hospital wound 3, Netanyahu vows 'iron fist' in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli drone strikes hit Kamal Adwan Hospital on Tuesday, wounding three medical staff at one of the few hospitals still partially operating in the northernmost part of Gaza, the facility’s director said. Dr. Hossam Abu Safiya said the drones were dropping bombs, spraying shrapnel at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden to attend reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris
item.description: First lady Jill Biden will travel to Paris on Saturday for the reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral, her office announced on Tuesday. She will already be abroad and travel from Doha, Qatar, to Paris that day before returning home to Washington on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: South Korean parliament blocks president's martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol attempted to declare martial law in the country for the first time in 45 years but was blocked by a parliament vote early Wednesday morning local time. He vowed to lift the order after hours of chaos and protests in the capital of Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel warns it will go after Lebanon directly if cease-fire with Hezbollah collapses
item.description: Israel issued a warning at Lebanon that it will not long distinguish between Beirut and Hezbollah should the cease-fire between Jerusalem and the terrorist group collapse following an exchange of strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China responds to US chip sanctions, banning exports of key minerals
item.description: China has banned exports of some rare minerals to the United States as tensions over trade heat up after the U.S. issued new restrictions on exports of certain semiconductor chips and equipment to China earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to attend reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he will attend the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral next weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Monday that investment banker Warren Stephens would serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Kingdom. “Warren has always dreamed of serving the United States full time. I am thrilled that he will now have that opportunity as the top Diplomat, representing the U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian tourist dies after being swept away by massive wave while doing yoga in Thailand
item.description: Russian tourist Kamilla Beliatskaya died on Friday after she was swept away by a large wave while doing yoga on a rock in Koh Samui, Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Putin leader backs harsh crackdown as over 40 people hospitalized amid protests in Georgia
item.description: Dozens are hospitalized after massive protests broke out across Georgia following the pro-Putin government's move halting decadeslong talks on joining the EU amid increasing ties with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan president speaks with US officials from Hawaii as China condemns visit
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te touched down in Hawaii over the weekend and spoke by phone with former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) as China slammed the U.S. for the visit and for selling weapons parts to Taipei. Hawaii Gov.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Largest gold deposit in the world worth $83 billion found in China
item.description: A discovery of gold reserves, estimated to be worth $83 billion, was made in China last month. China is the world's largest gold producer, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 15 after landslides bury 40 homes in eastern Uganda
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — At least 15 people have died and 113 others are missing after landslides buried homes in six villages in eastern Uganda, police said Thursday. Another 15 injured people have been rescued and admitted to Buluganya Health Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation
